sourceMovie.title: Martyrs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Martyrs works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Antichrist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Martyrs clicked for you, Antichrist (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Martyrs?
faq.answer: Try Antichrist, Frontier(s), The Girl Next Door—each shares DNA with Martyrs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Martyrs?
faq.answer: Martyrs (2008) is best described as Horror, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Martyrs worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
